slides.headline: Boost Your English Students' Thinking Speed Instantly
slides.headline_short: Boost Your English Students' Thinking Speed Instantly
slides.headline_display: Boost Your English Students' Thinking Speed Instantly

image_asset.local_path: C:\Users\User\OneDrive - Board of Education of SD 39 (Vancouver)\Documents\Carousels\carousels-repo2\.tmp\image-assets\studio-20260401-025354-91e376-v1\slide-01-pexels-8617760.jpeg
image_asset.credit: Photo by Yan Krukau on Pexels

slides.headline: Quick Word Associations
slides.body: Use simple prompts like 'apple' or 'happy' and ask students to quickly list related words. This warms up their brains for faster thinking.
slides.body_short: Use simple prompts like 'apple' or 'happy' and ask students to quickly list related words.
slides.body_display: Use simple prompts like 'apple' or 'happy' and ask students to quickly list related words. This warms up their brains for faster thinking.

image_asset.local_path: C:\Users\User\OneDrive - Board of Education of SD 39 (Vancouver)\Documents\Carousels\carousels-repo2\.tmp\image-assets\studio-20260401-025354-91e376-v1\slide-02-pexels-6502817.jpeg

slides.headline: Timed Story Starters
slides.body: Give students a sentence to start a story and limit their writing time to 2 minutes. This encourages spontaneous ideas and quick sentence formation.
slides.body_short: Give students a sentence to start a story and limit their writing time to 2 minutes.
slides.body_display: Give students a sentence to start a story and limit their writing time to 2 minutes. This encourages spontaneous ideas and quick sentence formation.

slides.headline: Picture Description Challenges
slides.body: Show a detailed image and have students describe as many elements as they can within 3 minutes to boost observation and quick vocabulary recall.
slides.body_short: Show a detailed image and have students describe as many elements as they can within 3
slides.body_display: Show a detailed image and have students describe as many elements as they can within 3 minutes to boost observation and quick vocabulary recall.

image_asset.local_path: C:\Users\User\OneDrive - Board of Education of SD 39 (Vancouver)\Documents\Carousels\carousels-repo2\.tmp\image-assets\studio-20260401-025354-91e376-v1\slide-04-pexels-6502817.jpeg

slides.headline: Speed Question Rounds
slides.body: Prepare rapid-fire questions on familiar topics and have students answer without overthinking, improving fluency and quick responses.
slides.body_short: Prepare rapid-fire questions on familiar topics and have students answer without overthinking
slides.body_display: Prepare rapid-fire questions on familiar topics and have students answer without overthinking, improving fluency and quick responses.

slides.headline: Word Chain Games
slides.body: Start with a word, and each student quickly adds a related word forming a chain. It's a fun way to practice vocabulary and quick thinking under pressure.
slides.body_short: Start with a word, and each student quickly adds a related word forming a chain.
slides.body_display: Start with a word, and each student quickly adds a related word forming a chain. It's a fun way to practice vocabulary and quick thinking under pressure.

slides.headline: Boost English Students' Thinking Speed with Low-Prep Writing Activities
slides.headline_short: Boost English Students' Thinking Speed with
slides.headline_display: Boost English Students' Thinking Speed with Low-Prep Writing Activities

image_asset.local_path: C:\Users\User\OneDrive - Board of Education of SD 39 (Vancouver)\Documents\Carousels\carousels-repo2\.tmp\image-assets\studio-20260401-025354-91e376-v2\slide-01-pexels-8617760.jpeg
image_asset.credit: Photo by Yan Krukau on Pexels

slides.headline: Quick Freewriting Sessions
slides.body: Set a timer for 3-5 minutes and ask students to write nonstop about a topic. This warms up their brains and builds fluency.
slides.body_short: Set a timer for 3-5 minutes and ask students to write nonstop about a topic.
slides.body_display: Set a timer for 3-5 minutes and ask students to write nonstop about a topic. This warms up their brains and builds fluency.

image_asset.local_path: C:\Users\User\OneDrive - Board of Education of SD 39 (Vancouver)\Documents\Carousels\carousels-repo2\.tmp\image-assets\studio-20260401-025354-91e376-v2\slide-02-pexels-6502817.jpeg

slides.headline: Sentence Expansion Challenges
slides.body: Give students simple sentences to expand by adding adjectives, adverbs, or details. This boosts creativity and speed.
slides.body_short: Give students simple sentences to expand by adding adjectives, adverbs, or details.
slides.body_display: Give students simple sentences to expand by adding adjectives, adverbs, or details. This boosts creativity and speed.

slides.headline: Timed Story Starters
slides.body: Provide a sentence prompt and have students write as much as they can in 5 minutes. It encourages rapid idea generation.
slides.body_short: Provide a sentence prompt and have students write as much as they can in 5 minutes.
slides.body_display: Provide a sentence prompt and have students write as much as they can in 5 minutes. It encourages rapid idea generation.

image_asset.local_path: C:\Users\User\OneDrive - Board of Education of SD 39 (Vancouver)\Documents\Carousels\carousels-repo2\.tmp\image-assets\studio-20260401-025354-91e376-v2\slide-04-pexels-6502817.jpeg

slides.headline: Collaborative Chain Writing
slides.body: Students write sentences in turns, building a story together quickly. This promotes fast thinking and teamwork.
slides.body_short: Students write sentences in turns, building a story together quickly.
slides.body_display: Students write sentences in turns, building a story together quickly. This promotes fast thinking and teamwork.

slides.headline: Quick Question Prompts
slides.body: Ask students rapid-fire questions to respond in writing, improving their quick recall and writing agility.
slides.body_short: Ask students rapid-fire questions to respond in writing
slides.body_display: Ask students rapid-fire questions to respond in writing, improving their quick recall and writing agility.
